events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need interpret scenario: rigging configuration, sling angle under 30°, tension math - ME. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. In rigging, sling angle less than 30 degrees is generally prohibited because tension increases dramatically; ASME B30 and OSHA require minimum angle? OSHA 1926.251(c)(4) says sling shall not be loaded in excess of rated capacity; rating reduced by angle. ASME B30.9 says slings should be rigged to avoid angles less than 30°? Many standards recommend minimum 30° angle between sling and horizontal? Actually angle to horizontal less than 30° causes high load multiplier. If sling angle under 30° (likely angle with horizontal) is out of tolerance. Decision fail, risk high/critical? Sling angle under 30° can produce tension more than double vertical load; potential for sling overload and failure. Risk high or critical. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80. value numeric reading: angle in degrees, e.g., 25. confidence 0.9. findings: sling angle below 30° minimum, tension multiplier excessive, sling capacity exceeded. actions: re-rig to increase sling angle above 30°, use spreader bar/lifting beam, verify capacity with angle correction. rationale. references: ASME B30.9, OSHA 1926.251(c)(4). Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside JSON.

    "Sling angle is estimated below 30°, which is outside acceptable rigging practice.",
    "Tension in each sling leg increases sharply as the angle decreases, creating a load multiplier that can exceed the sling's rated capacity.",
    "Configuration is non-compliant with safe rigging requirements for sling angles."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-rig the load to increase the sling angle to at least 30° or greater.",
    "Use a spreader bar or lifting beam if the geometry cannot be improved by rigging alone.",
    "Recalculate sling tension and verify rated capacity is not exceeded before lifting.",
    "Have a qualified rigger inspect and approve the revised configuration."
  ],
  "rationale": "A sling angle under 30° produces a tension multiplier greater than 2.0 times the vertical load per leg, which can overload the slings and lead to failure. ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require rigging to be configured so that slings are not loaded beyond their rated capacity; the observed low-angle condition violates this requirement.",

    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251(c)(4)"
  ]
